turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've had a persistent cough for about five days now, and it started out dry but has become productive with yellowish-green sputum. To be honest, I'm getting really worried because it doesn't seem to be getting any better.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: Yes, it's been constant and it's hard for me to sleep or concentrate on work. I've also been taking OTC dextromethorphan to try and calm it down, but it hasn't really helped much.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: I've had some trouble breathing too, especially when I'm exerting myself or lying down. It feels like my chest is tight and it's hard to catch my breath, even after a short walk.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: Actually, I have been experiencing night sweats, soaking through my pajamas every night for four nights now. It's really unsettled me and made me even more worried about what might be going on with my body.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: The cough, it's mostly in my chest and when I breathe deeply it feels like it's moving up into my throat. And sometimes it hurts a bit in my back too, but not always.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: I've been feeling pretty fatigued and just generally unwell, to be honest. It's hard for me to explain, but I feel really drained and my body just feels weak all over.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. But to be honest, I have been feeling pretty tired and run down lately, not just from this cough, but also because I've been missing work and haven't had much of an appetite either.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. Actually, one thing that has surprised me is how little appetite I've had over the past few days. Even thinking about food makes me a bit queasy, but I wouldn't say I've felt nauseous or gotten stomach upset in the way you're asking.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: I'd have to say my energy levels have been really low, and I've missed three days of work already because I just didn't feel up to it. As for my appetite, I'd say I've had a decreased desire to eat over the past five days - even things I normally enjoy don't sound appealing to me right now.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: Yes, actually, I've had a low-grade fever that comes and goes. It's been around 38.2°C (100.8°F), intermittent, and it gets worse in the evenings. And like we already talked about, the night sweats have been really concerning for me - soaking through my pajamas every night for four nights now.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I don't think I have any other serious medical conditions that could be related to this cough and fatigue. But, I did have a normal physical exam about a year ago, everything seemed fine then. And, just to mention it, I'm allergic to penicillin - it causes me to break out in a rash.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've just been trying to take some OTC dextromethorphan for my cough as needed, but it hasn't really helped much. The packaging says to take 15-30mg every 4-6 hours, and I think I might have been taking a bit more than that because the cough was getting so bad. But like I said, it's not really working.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: I'd say my diet is pretty normal, I'm an elementary school teacher so I have to stay energized throughout the day. Usually, I have breakfast, like cereal or toast with fruit, and then maybe a snack in the afternoon, like yogurt or crackers. I've been trying to eat something small before bed, but even that hasn't sounded appealing lately, which is weird for me because I normally love cooking and trying new recipes.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been trying to stay active, but it's been tough lately. As an elementary school teacher, I do have to move around a lot when I'm at work, so that helps a bit. But when I get home, I just feel so exhausted and drained, even doing something as simple as going for a short walk feels like too much effort.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My mom actually has asthma, she's been managing it for years with inhalers and medication. She's been a good role model for me when it comes to taking care of my own health, so I've always been pretty proactive about getting checked out if something doesn't feel right.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yes, to be honest, I do worry about my own health a lot more now, especially with this cough and fatigue. It's hard not to think about my mom's asthma and wonder if there might be something similar going on with me. The uncertainty is really unsettling for me.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I'm a bit surprised by that question, but I think it's fair. To answer, I do occasionally have a glass of wine on weekends when I go out with friends or my partner and I. It's not every week, maybe 1-2 times a month. As for stress, I try to cope by taking things one step at a time, focusing on my work and spending time with loved ones. Lately, though, it's been tough because of this cough and fatigue – it feels like everything is just piling up on me.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke at all.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I appreciate your reassurance that there's no judgment. Honestly, one thing that's been on my mind but hasn't felt like it was directly related to my cough is that several students in my class have recently had upper respiratory infections. I've been worried about getting exposed and bringing something home to my partner or even possibly spreading it to others at school. It might sound silly, but as an elementary school teacher, I feel a sense of responsibility for keeping everyone healthy, especially the children in my care.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: To be honest, one thing that keeps coming back to me is how this could affect my work and my students. As a teacher, I'm used to being on top of things and being there for everyone, but with this cough and fatigue, I'm worried about not being able to do my job to the best of my ability, especially during cold and flu season when so many kids are coming down with illnesses.
